description_long: This is a cylindrical, red fire extinguisher with a slightly domed top and a flat base, showing signs of wear and grime, especially near the bottom. The extinguisher features a prominent black hose attached near the top, looping down and secured into a molded black holder at the base. A red lever and nozzle assembly are mounted on the top, with the lever angled outward and the nozzle pointing away from the cylinder. Faint white instructional markings and symbols are visible on one side of the cylinder, though they are partially worn and difficult to read. The base is encased in a black protective ring, likely rubber or plastic, which provides stability and impact resistance. The overall appearance suggests it is designed for use in public or industrial urban environments, such as garages, sidewalks, or building corridors.
description: A worn, red cylindrical fire extinguisher with a black hose, red lever, and black base ring, featuring faded white instructions on one side.
description_view_0: The front view shows the red cylinder, black hose attached at the top and secured in a black holder at the base, and the red lever and nozzle assembly clearly visible.
description_view_1: This angled view highlights the smooth, slightly domed top and the red lever, with the hose partially visible and the faded white label just barely discernible.
description_view_2: The side view displays the faded white instructional markings most clearly, with the hose and black base ring also visible.
description_view_3: The rear view shows the hose fully looped and secured in the black holder, with the back of the red cylinder and base ring exposed.
attribute_label_or_signage: Faded white instructional label with text and symbols on the side of the cylinder.
attribute_mounting_bracket_or_holder: A black molded holder is attached at the base, securing the hose.
attribute_hose_or_nozzle: A black hose is attached near the top, approximately the height of the cylinder, looping down and secured in the base holder; a red nozzle is attached to the top.
